comment_text.sn35.69:1.1: The commentary says this was Upasena son of Vaṅgantā, Sāriputta’s younger brother. When newly ordained, he made the mistake of ordaining students too early (Kd 1:31.3.2), but later developed an impressive following (Bu NP 15:1.1.9), leading the Buddha to declare him the foremost of those who were “impressive all around” (AN 1.213). His song of triumph is recorded at Ud 4.9, while his verses at Thag 10.6 focus on contentment and humility.
comment_text.sn35.69:1.4: This illustrates the difference between bāhira (“exterior”) and bahiddha (“external, outside”). Bāhira is the outside of an organism or a single thing (such as an altar), whereas bahiddha is something outside a point of reference or removed past a boundary. That is, interior/exterior are two aspects of a single thing, whereas inside/outside are two distinct things.

root_text.sn35.69:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca upaseno rājagahe viharanti sītavane sappasoṇḍikapabbhāre.
root_text.sn35.69:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmato upasenassa kāye āsīviso patito hoti.
root_text.sn35.69:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā upaseno bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn35.69:1.4: “etha me, āvuso, imaṁ kāyaṁ mañcakaṁ āropetvā bahiddhā nīharatha.
root_text.sn35.69:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ upasenaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.69:2.2: “na kho pana mayaṁ passāma āyasmato upasenassa kāyassa vā aññathattaṁ indriyānaṁ vā vipariṇāmaṁ.
root_text.sn35.69:2.4: ‘etha me, āvuso, imaṁ kāyaṁ mañcakaṁ āropetvā bahiddhā nīharatha.
root_text.sn35.69:2.9: ahaṁ jivhāti vā mama jivhāti vā …
root_text.sn35.69:2.10: ahaṁ manoti vā mama mano’ti vā.
root_text.sn35.69:2.11: Tassa, āvuso sāriputta, siyā kāyassa vā aññathattaṁ indriyānaṁ vā vipariṇāmo.
root_text.sn35.69:2.12: Mayhañca kho, āvuso sāriputta, na evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn35.69:2.14: ahaṁ jivhāti vā mama jivhāti vā …pe…
root_text.sn35.69:2.15: ahaṁ manoti vā mama manoti vā’.
root_text.sn35.69:2.16: Tassa mayhañca kho, āvuso sāriputta, kiṁ kāyassa vā aññathattaṁ bhavissati, indriyānaṁ vā vipariṇāmo”ti.
root_text.sn35.69:3.1: Tathā hi panāyasmato upasenassa dīgharattaṁ ahaṅkāramamaṅkāramānānusayo susamūhato.
root_text.sn35.69:3.2: Tasmā āyasmato upasenassa na evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn35.69:3.4: ahaṁ jivhāti vā mama jivhāti vā …pe…
root_text.sn35.69:3.5: ahaṁ manoti vā mama mano’ti vā”ti.
root_text.sn35.69:3.6: Atha kho te bhikkhū āyasmato upasenassa kāyaṁ mañcakaṁ āropetvā bahiddhā nīhariṁsu.
root_text.sn35.69:3.7: Atha kho āyasmato upasenassa kāyo tattheva vikiri;

translation_text.sn35.69:1.1: At one time the venerables Sāriputta and Upasena were staying near Rājagaha in the Cool Grove, under the Snake’s Hood Grotto.
translation_text.sn35.69:1.2: Now at that time a viper fell on Upasena’s body.
translation_text.sn35.69:1.4: “Come, reverends, lift this body onto a cot and carry it outside
translation_text.sn35.69:2.1: When he said this, Sāriputta said to him,
translation_text.sn35.69:2.2: “But we don’t see any impairment in your body or deterioration of your faculties.
translation_text.sn35.69:2.4: ‘Come, reverends, lift this body onto a cot and carry it outside
translation_text.sn35.69:2.7: “Reverend Sāriputta, there may be an impairment in body or deterioration of faculties for someone who thinks:
translation_text.sn35.69:2.8: ‘I am the eye’ or ‘the eye is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.69:2.9: Or ‘I am the ear … nose … tongue … body …’
translation_text.sn35.69:2.10: Or ‘I am the mind’ or ‘the mind is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.69:2.12: But I don’t think like that.
translation_text.sn35.69:2.16: So why would there be an impairment in my body or deterioration of my faculties?”
translation_text.sn35.69:3.1: “That must be because Venerable Upasena has long ago totally eradicated I-making, mine-making, and the underlying tendency to conceit.
translation_text.sn35.69:3.2: That’s why it doesn’t occur to you:
translation_text.sn35.69:3.3: ‘I am the eye’ or ‘the eye is mine.’ Or ‘I am the ear …
translation_text.sn35.69:3.5: Or ‘I am the mind’ or ‘the mind is mine.’”
translation_text.sn35.69:3.6: Then those mendicants lifted Upasena’s body onto a cot and carried it outside.
translation_text.sn35.69:3.7: And his body was scattered right there
